zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor of England Countess of Bar於1269年6月18日出生在英國波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素時，她的父親Edward I King of England30歲，母親Eleanor of Castile Queen of England29歲。她於1290年8月15日與Alphonso III di Aragona Rey de Aragón, Valencia e Sicilia在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比結婚。她去世於1298年10月12日在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特享年29歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: England
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor of England Countess of Bar was born on 18 June 1269, in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, her father, Edward I King of England, was 30 and her mother, Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, was 29. She married Alphonso III di Aragona Rey de Aragón, Valencia e Sicilia on 15 August 1290, in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England. She died on 12 October 1298, in Gent, East Flanders, Belgium, at the age of 29, and was buried in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor of England Countess of Bar est née le 18 juin 1269, à Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Edward I King of England, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Alphonso III di Aragona Rey de Aragón, Valencia e Sicilia le 15 août 1290, à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 12 octobre 1298, à Gent, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, à l’âge de 29 ans, et a été enterrée à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London,: Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor of England Countess of Bar am 18. Juni 1269 in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edward I King of England 30 und ihre Mutter Eleanor of Castile Queen of England 29. Sie hat am 15. August 1290 in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England Alphonso III di Aragona Rey de Aragón, Valencia e Sicilia geheiratet. Sie starb am 12. Oktober 1298 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Gent, Ostflandern, Belgien und wurde in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor of England Countess of Bar è nata il il 18 giugno 1269 nel Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, sua padre, Edward I King of England, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Alphonso III di Aragona Rey de Aragón, Valencia e Sicilia il 15 agosto 1290, nel Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England. Morì il 12 ottobre 1298, nel Gent, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, all'età di 29 anni, e fu sepolta a Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor of England Countess of Bar(이)가 1269년 6월 18일 영국 버크실르 윈드소르 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward I King of England(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor of Castile Queen of England(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1290년 8월 15일 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에서 Alphonso III di Aragona Rey de Aragón, Valencia e Sicilia(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1298년 10월 12일 벨기에 스트-블라아더 젠트에서 29세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor of England Countess of Bar nasceu em 18 de junho de 1269, em Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward I King of England, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Alphonso III di Aragona Rey de Aragón, Valencia e Sicilia em 15 de agosto de 1290, em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra. ru.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London,: вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor of England Countess of Bar родилась 18 июня 1269 г. в местности виндзор кастл, биркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edward I King of England было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alphonso III di Aragona Rey de Aragón, Valencia e Sicilia 15 августа 1290 г. в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия. Она умерла 12 октября 1298 г. в местности джент, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия в возрасте 29 лет и была похоронена в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия.

Se casó con Alphonso III di Aragona Rey de Aragón, Valencia e Sicilia el 15 de agosto de 1290, en Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Murió el 12 de octubre de 1298, en Gent, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, a la edad de 29 años, y fue sepultada en Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
